data.title: Characterization of Bronchodilator Response in Children With Bronchiolitis Using Phenotypic and Genotypic Features
data.brief_summary: Bronchiolitis is the leading cause of pediatric morbidity and healthcare costs. Despite the commonplace use of bronchodilator treatments, like albuterol, in conditions like asthma, their efficacy in bronchiolitis remains controversial due to the heterogeneity in patient response. Although studies indicate that bronchodilators do not enhance outcomes in bronchiolitis, meta-analyses can obscure the heterogeneity of treatment effects. Inclusion Criteria:

* Children between 3 to 24 months of age
* Clinical diagnosis of bronchiolitis by the treating provider(s), defined by the American Academy of Pediatrics as a clinical syndrome involving lower respiratory tract symptoms

data.title: A Randomized, Controlled, Open-label Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Ferrlecit® vs Oral Iron in Iron Deficient Patients With Chronic Kidney Disease Being Treated With Erythropoietic Therapy
data.brief_summary: This study compares the effect of Ferrlecit® (a form of intravenous iron) to ferrous sulfate (a form of oral iron) in treating anemia and iron deficiency in chronic kidney disease patients who are receiving erythropoietic agents, such as Procrit® and Aranesp®.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Moderate to severe anemia
* Iron deficiency
* Moderate to severe chronic kidney disease
* Receiving therapy with erythropoietic agent

Exclusion Criteria:

* Receiving dialysis
* Known sensitivity to Ferrlecit® or any of its components
* Clinically unstable

data.title: A Randomized, Controlled, Open-label Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Ferrlecit® vs Oral Iron in Iron Deficient Patients With Chronic Kidney Disease
data.brief_summary: This study compares the effect of Ferrlecit® (a form of intravenous iron) to ferrous sulfate (a form of oral iron) in treating anemia and iron deficiency in chronic kidney disease patients who are not receiving erythropoietic agents (hormones that stimulate the bone marrow to make more red blood cells).
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Moderate to severe anemia
* Iron deficiency
* Moderate to severe chronic kidney disease

Exclusion Criteria:

* Receiving dialysis
* Known sensitivity to Ferrlecit® or any of its components
* Receiving therapy with erythropoietic agent
* Clinically unstable

data.title: Clinical Evaluation of GW815SF for Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (Chronic Bronchitis, Emphysema)" A Long-term Treatment Study of GW815SF50/500µg in Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease -
data.brief_summary: This study evaluates the safety of medicine on COPD (Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease). This study will last up to 56 weeks, and subjects will visit the clinic 16 times. Subjects will be given breathing tests, and will record their breathing symptoms daily on diary cards.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion criteria:

* Diagnosis of COPD.

Exclusion criteria:

* Diagnosis of asthma or an uncontrolled medical condition or respiratory disorder other than COPD.
* Other inclusion and exclusion criteria will be evaluated at the first study visit.
endpoints: Number of Participants With Any Adverse Events (AEs) and Serious AEs (SAEs)

data.title: Subpopulations and Intermediate Outcome Measures in COPD Study
data.brief_summary:
SPIROMICS I, SPIROMICS II, and SPIROMICS III are longitudinal observational studies of Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) cohort.

SPIROMICS I had two primary aims: (1) To find groups of patients with COPD who share certain characteristics; (2) To find new ways of measuring whether or not COPD is getting worse and to provide new ways of testing whether a new treatment is working.

Inclusion Criteria:

* Between 40 and 80 at baseline visit

* Current or former smokers: \>20 pack-year history of smoking
